questions.prompt: In what price range are you looking to buy?
options: Under $400k
$1.5M and above

questions.prompt: How soon would you like to be settled in your new home?
options: Within 3 months - I have a deadline or I am ready now
3 to 6 months - I am actively preparing
6 to 12 months - I am planning ahead so I am not rushed
No fixed timeline - I will know the right situation when I see it

questions.prompt: What type of home are you looking for?
options: Single-family home
Condo or townhome
Luxury or high-end property
Land
Multi-family property
I am open or not sure yet

questions.prompt: How would you describe your level of experience with buying a home?
options: This is my first time - I want someone who explains everything and never assumes I already know
I have done this before - I understand the basics but want a strong guide for this specific market
I am very experienced - I need sharp execution and market access, not education

questions.prompt: When you picture working with your ideal agent, what does that relationship look like?
options: A trusted advisor who stays ahead of me and brings me what I need before I ask
A thinking partner - someone I reason through decisions with as equals
A skilled executor - I set the direction; I need someone who delivers precisely and quickly

questions.prompt: How do you prefer to handle quick back-and-forth communications during your transaction?
options: Text - fast, easy, I can respond whenever
Phone - I'd rather just talk it through quickly
Either is fine - whatever works best in the moment

questions.prompt: How do you prefer to receive updates, timelines, and documents?
options: Email - I like having everything in writing I can refer back to
Text with attachments - quick delivery, I'll open it right away
Phone call first, then email recap - I want to talk through it and have a record

questions.prompt: When you are close to making a big decision, what do you need most to move forward?
options: The numbers - show me the comps, the trends, the math, and I will make my call
Time and space - I do not make big decisions quickly and I do not want to be rushed
A trusted perspective - I want someone experienced to tell me what they would do in my shoes
A gut feeling - the data matters, but ultimately, I need to feel right about it

questions.prompt: You just lost a bidding war on a home you loved. What do you need from your agent at that moment?
options: Give me the facts and options immediately - I process by analyzing what happened and planning the next move
Help me step back - I need space to process the disappointment before making another decision
Reassure me - remind me that this was not the only home, and we will find the right one
Stay calm and steady - do not over-explain, do not panic, just be a grounding presence

questions.prompt: What is non-negotiable in your agent?
options: Market expert - know this market cold
Fierce negotiator - fights for every dollar
Responds fast - I won't wonder where they are
Calm & low-drama - keeps it smooth start to finish
My interests first - always puts me ahead of the agent's interests
Honest & straightforward - tells me hard truths

questions.prompt: How involved do you want to be in the details of buying your home?
options: Very involved - I want to weigh in on every decision
Keep me informed on what matters, but I trust my agent to handle the details
Hands off - tell me what you need from me and take care of the rest

questions.prompt: When it comes to choosing an agent, which matters more to you?
options: An agent whose broad market connections give you access others cannot - even if those connections sometimes come with competing loyalties
An agent whose only job is representing you, with no other relationships that could compromise your deal

questions.prompt: When you reach out to your agent, how quickly do you expect a response?
options: Within 10 minutes - I need fast turnaround, especially in competitive situations
Within 30 minutes - reasonably quick, I do not like waiting long
Within a few hours - same day is fine as long as it is prompt
Within 24 hours - I am patient and understand agents are busy

questions.prompt: How do you plan to handle commissions to your buyer's agent?
options: I plan to negotiate - I have done my research and I know what I want to pay
I am open to it but want to understand my options first
I would rather find the right agent first and work out commissions later
I do not fully understand how buyer agent commissions work yet - I want to learn

questions.prompt: What do you estimate your home is worth?
options: Under $400k
$1.5M and above

questions.prompt: How soon do you need your home on the market?
options: Within 4 weeks - I need to move fast
1 to 3 months - I want to prepare properly and do this right
3 to 6 months - I am planning ahead
No rush - I will list when the situation is right

questions.prompt: What type of property are you selling?
options: Single-family home
Condo or townhome
Luxury or high-end property
Land
Multi-family property
Other

questions.prompt: What is driving this sale?
options: Moving up or downsizing - it is a planned lifestyle change
Relocating for work or a major life change
Financial pressure or estate settlement
It feels like the right time - no major urgency
A major personal transition - divorce, separation, loss, or a difficult chapter
Other

questions.prompt: Every seller wants a strong price. But when you imagine this sale going well, what does that actually look like?
options: Maximum price, even if it means patience and time on the market
A strong price AND a smooth, low-stress process from start to finish
Speed and certainty - I want this resolved on clear terms
I need to close by a specific date - the deadline is more important than maximizing price

questions.prompt: How involved do you want to be in the details of selling your home?
options: Very involved - I want to weigh in on pricing strategy, marketing, staging, and negotiation decisions
Keep me informed on what matters, but I trust my agent to handle the details
Hands off - tell me what you need from me and take care of the rest

questions.prompt: How do you prefer to handle quick back-and-forth communications during your transaction?
options: Text - fast, easy, I can respond whenever
Phone - I'd rather just talk it through quickly
Either is fine - whatever works best in the moment

questions.prompt: How do you prefer to receive updates, timelines, and documents?
options: Email - I like having everything in writing I can refer back to
Text with attachments - quick delivery, I'll open it right away
Phone call first, then email recap - I want to talk through it and have a record

questions.prompt: Imagine your agent truly delivered. When you look back on the experience, what made you feel that way?
options: Priced it right - attracted strong offers
Great marketing - made my home look exceptional
Great negotiated outcomes - better deal than I could get
Reachable & responsive - always there when I needed
Kept the process calm - no drama, no surprises
Honest & straightforward - told me hard truths

questions.prompt: How would you describe your connection to this home?
options: It is an asset - I want the best financial outcome and a clean process
I have good memories here but I am ready to move on
This home is part of my identity - selling it is a real loss, even if it is the right decision
It is complicated - this sale is tied to something emotionally difficult

questions.prompt: When you're not hearing from your agent, what do you prefer?
options: Regular scheduled check-ins - I like knowing when to expect updates
Updates at key milestones - only when something meaningful happens
I'll reach out when I need something - I do not want to be over-communicated to

questions.prompt: When it comes to choosing an agent, which matters more to you?
options: An agent whose broad market connections give you access others cannot - even if those connections sometimes come with competing loyalties
An agent whose only job is representing you, with no other relationships that could compromise your deal

questions.prompt: When you reach out to your agent, how quickly do you expect a response?
options: Within 10 minutes - I need fast turnaround, especially in time-sensitive situations
Within 30 minutes - reasonably quick, I do not like waiting long
Within a few hours - same day is fine as long as it is prompt
Within 24 hours - I am patient and understand agents are busy

questions.prompt: How do you plan to handle commissions to your listing agent?
options: I plan to negotiate - I have done my research and I know what I want to pay
I am open to the conversation but I want to understand my options first
I will pay a fair rate for the right agent - commissions are not my primary concern
I do not fully understand how seller-side commissions work yet - I want to learn

questions.prompt: Which side of the transaction do you primarily represent?
options: Buyer representation
Seller representation

questions.prompt: What is your typical price range?
options: Under $400k
$1.5M and above

questions.prompt: Where do you do your best work?
options: First-time buyers who need guidance through the entire process
Move-up or downsizing buyers navigating a transition
Relocation clients on tight timelines
Luxury transactions with high-touch expectations
Investors focused on numbers and execution
Land or multi-family transactions
Sellers and listings
Condos and townhomes
Other

questions.prompt: How would most of your clients describe working with you?
options: Strategic and data-driven - I bring the analysis, the market context, and a clear plan
Calm and steady - I keep things grounded and drama-free, especially when it gets stressful
Warm and relational - my clients feel genuinely cared for as people, not just as transactions
Efficient and decisive - I move fast, communicate clearly, and do not waste their time

questions.prompt: How often do you communicate with clients during a transaction?
options: I check in regularly on a set schedule, whether or not there is news to share
I update clients at every key milestone and decision point
I let clients set the pace - I am responsive when they reach out but I do not initiate unless something requires their attention

questions.prompt: How do you prefer to handle quick back-and-forth with clients during a transaction?
options: Text - fast and easy, clients can reach me anytime
Phone - I'd rather just talk it through quickly
Either is fine - I adapt to what works for the client

questions.prompt: How do you typically deliver updates, timelines, and documents to clients?
options: Email - everything documented, easy to reference later
Text with attachments - quick delivery, clients get it right away
Phone call first, then email recap - I talk through it and follow up in writing

questions.prompt: When a deal gets difficult - bad inspection, appraisal gap, unreasonable counterparty - what is your instinct?
options: Get the client the facts immediately and lay out every option so they can decide
Slow it down - make sure the client fully understands the situation before anyone reacts
I take the lead - I work the problem, protect my client from unnecessary noise, and bring them in when there is a real decision to make
De-escalate first - bring the temperature down, then problem-solve together

questions.prompt: When a client reaches out, how quickly do you typically respond?
options: Within 10 minutes
Within 30 minutes
Within a few hours - same day always
Within 24 hours

questions.prompt: How do you approach commission conversations with clients?
options: I bring it up proactively and my rate is set - I explain my value and it speaks for itself
I bring it up proactively and I am open to discussing it based on the client and transaction
I wait for the client to raise it and my rate is set - if they ask I explain my value
I wait for the client to raise it and I am happy to have that conversation openly

questions.prompt: You are the listing agent and an unrepresented buyer walks in interested in your listing. What do you do?
options: I refer the buyer to an agent at a completely separate brokerage - they deserve independent representation
I represent the seller only and the buyer proceeds unrepresented - with full written disclosure to all parties
Another agent at my brokerage steps in to represent the buyer - with full written disclosure to both parties

questions.prompt: Who are you NOT the right fit for?

proposedScoringLogic.status: To be determined with development team

inputs.name: Category ranking
inputs.description: Consumer and agent each rank the four categories 1 through 4 in order of importance. This ranking becomes a weight applied to that category's questions in the match score. First-ranked category matters most. Fourth-ranked matters least.

inputs.name: Core question answers
inputs.description: Each answer choice is pre-tagged on the back end to a position within its category. Consumer answer and agent answer are compared per question. Questions within a category are aggregated into a category fit score. All four category scores are weighted by the ranking and combined into an overall bilateral fit score.

inputs.name: Pax AI Deep Dive
inputs.description: Pax knows which categories the consumer ranked highest and asks behavioral follow-up questions targeting those categories. The Pax responses refine the category scores for the top-ranked categories, producing a more precise profile than the core questions alone.
